title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth's concert at MGM Music Hall featured a mix of jazz, pop, and nostalgia from his "Whatever's Clever!" tour.
Puth, a Berklee alum, emphasized musicianship through stripped-down performances, showcasing his piano skills and vocal control.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey captivated the audience with indie-pop sounds and live looping performances, respectively.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed their entire 'Bringing Down the Horse' album at a sold-out show at The Cabot in Beverly.
Jakob Dylan and the band followed their album performance with a full rendition of Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark'.
Encore included Petty classics 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting', highlighting Dylan's admiration for Petty's music.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, continuing its population decline since 2020, now totaling a 5,644 decrease.
Everett gained the most residents in Massachusetts last year, with an increase of 2,362, followed by Worcester with 1,916.
Massachusetts' population grew by 15,500 people, a 0.2% increase, with slower growth due to high housing costs and reduced immigration.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments, led by the Johnson family, ranked 14th in Forbes' largest U.S. family businesses list.
Demoulas Super Markets, earning $8 billion annually, placed 69th in Forbes' list of major family-run firms.
Forbes ranked firms by revenue with founding families in significant roles; both public and private companies were included.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for a major housing redevelopment project.
The project aims to modernize campus housing, expand amenities, and maintain affordability for students.
Redevelopment will occur in phases, allowing student occupancy during renovations.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at Chevalier Theatre in Medford on May 22, 2026.
Williams' setlist included topical protest songs from her albums 'World's Gone Wrong' and 'Good Souls Better Angels'.
The concert featured covers of Bob Marley, Memphis Minnie, The Beatles, Skip James, and Neil Young.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle struck out nine but was pulled after six innings against the Twins.
Reliever Justin Slaten gave up four hits and four runs in less than an inning, including two home runs.
Boston's offense scored six but could not overcome Minnesota's 8-6 win, leaving two runners stranded in the eighth.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the ceremonial first pitch to his son Kody Clemens at Fenway Park on Friday.
Clemens wore a custom No. 21 'Rocket Man' Red Sox jersey for the Red Sox vs. Twins game.
Kody Clemens, playing for the Twins, previously hit a home run in his first Fenway game last year.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to NBA All-Defensive First Team for his performance during the 2025-26 season.
White recorded 98 blocks and 88 steals, becoming the fourth Celtics player to achieve these numbers.
The Celtics finished second in Eastern Conference with a 56-26 record, despite Jayson Tatum's absence.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: ‘Glory’, a Civil War drama, is now streaming on Netflix until June 1.
Tatiana Maslany stars in ‘Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed’ on Apple TV, a show about a suburban mom delving into crime.
The animated film ‘Arco’ is available on Hulu, featuring voice work from Mark Ruffalo and Natalie Portman.
